rationale: In the citing article, the Shiv, Carmon, and Ariely (2005a) paper appears only as part of a grouped parenthetical citation (with 2005b) in a brief statement about marketing action-induced expectancies. The paper is used as a general example of the marketing placebo-effects topic, not as a source whose specific findings, methods, or mechanisms are developed in detail. The citing article does not build hypotheses, analyses, or claims directly on this specific study, so the citation functions as background mention rather than a substantive linkage.
